Title: Some users may see that when selecting a meeting room when scheduling meetings, it changes from available to unavailable

More info: While we are working to remediate the impact, admins can mitigate the issue by assigning the desired working hours and the time zone for the meeting rooms.

Current status: Our reviews and checks on the stability and reliability of the aforementioned fix are progressing, though taking an extended period of time as we continue our efforts to ensure it fully addresses impact. Additionally, we anticipate that we will be able to provide an estimated remediation timeline by our next scheduled update. 

Scope of impact: Any users that are trying to select a meeting room when scheduling meetings outside the room's working hours in Exchange Online may be impacted. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

issues.title: Admins' search parameters may be missing after performing a group search in the Exchange admin center
issues.userImpact: Admins' search parameters may have been missing after performing a group search in the Exchange admin center.
issues.scopeOfImpact:
Any admin who performed a group search in the Exchange admin center may have been affected.

Start time: Tuesday, April 14, 2026, at 11:37 AM UTC

End time: Wednesday, April 22, 2026, at 12:00 AM UTC
issues.rootCause:
A recent service-side configuration change contained a code issue that prevented the search parameters from populating after performing a group search in the Exchange admin center.

Next steps: We're further analyzing the offending configuration change to find ways to improve our pre-deployment testing and validation processes and to prevent similar problems in the future. 

This is the final update for the event.

posts.content:
Title: Admins' search parameters may be missing after performing a group search in the Exchange admin center

User impact: Admins' search parameters may have been missing after performing a group search in the Exchange admin center.

More info: Specifically, the search terms were removed upon initiating group searches.

Other searches didn't appear to be affected.

Final status: Following our completed fix deployment, we've confirmed with affected admins that search parameters when conducting group searches in the Exchange Online admin center have been restored, and that the impact has been remediated. 

Scope of impact: Any admin who performed a group search in the Exchange admin center may have been affected.

Start time: Tuesday, April 14, 2026, at 11:37 AM UTC

End time: Wednesday, April 22, 2026, at 12:00 AM UTC

Root cause: A recent service-side configuration change contained a code issue that prevented the search parameters from populating after performing a group search in the Exchange admin center.

Next steps: We're further analyzing the offending configuration change to find ways to improve our pre-deployment testing and validation processes and to prevent similar problems in the future. 

This is the final update for the event.

issues.title: Admins may see stale or delayed report data in the Microsoft 365 admin center
issues.userImpact: Admins may see stale or delayed report data in the Microsoft 365 admin center.
issues.scopeOfImpact:
Any admins located in the North America, Asia-Pacific, and Europe regions who are attempting to view-up-to-date report data for the listed reports in the More info for Monday, May 11, 2026, or Tuesday, May 12, 2026, in the Microsoft 365 admin center may be affected by this issue. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Friday, May 15, 2026, at 12:00 AM UTC
issues.rootCause: A portion of infrastructure that supports and provides report data to the Microsoft 365 admin center is performing below expected thresholds is leading to report data being stale and delayed.

posts.content:
Title: Admins may see stale or delayed report data in the Microsoft 365 admin center

User impact: Admins may see stale or delayed report data in the Microsoft 365 admin center.

More info: The affected reports for Monday, May 11, 2026, or Tuesday, May 12, 2026, include the following:
- Copilot Readiness Activity
- Microsoft 365 App Usage
- Project Activity
- Visio Activity

Current status: We're continuing to monitor the progress of processing the backlog of report data for Tuesday, May 12, 2026, which is taking longer than anticipated to complete. Once available, we'll provide a resolution timeline.

Scope of impact: Any admins located in the North America, Asia-Pacific, and Europe regions who are attempting to view-up-to-date report data for the listed reports in the More info for Monday, May 11, 2026, or Tuesday, May 12, 2026, in the Microsoft 365 admin center may be affected by this issue. This section may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Friday, May 15, 2026, at 12:00 AM UTC

Root cause: A portion of infrastructure that supports and provides report data to the Microsoft 365 admin center is performing below expected thresholds is leading to report data being stale and delayed.

Next update by: Monday, May 18, 2026, at 5:30 PM UTC

Title: Admins may see stale or delayed report data in the Microsoft 365 admin center

User impact: Admins may see stale or delayed report data in the Microsoft 365 admin center.

 
More info: The affected reports for Monday, May 11, 2026, or Tuesday, May 12, 2026, include the following:
- Copilot Readiness Activity
- Microsoft 365 App Usage
- Project Activity
- Visio Activity
 
Current status: We’ve been made aware that while initially this issue affected the four listed affected data reports for Monday 11, 2026, increased traffic to the portion of infrastructure that supports and provides report data to the Microsoft 365 admin center has additionally impacted these reports for Tuesday, May 12, 2026. We’ve updated the More info and Scope of impact to align with our findings. history.title: Users are intermittently unable to access Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat using the Microsoft Edge browser
history.userImpact: Users were intermittently unable to access Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat using the Microsoft Edge browser.

posts.content: A post-incident report has been published.

Title: Users are intermittently unable to access Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat using the Microsoft Edge browser

User impact: Users were intermittently unable to access Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat using the Microsoft Edge browser.

More info: Users were reporting intermittent "Bad Request" errors when accessing m365.cloud.microsoft using the Microsoft Edge browser.

Final status: We've confirmed with previously affected users that rolling back the offending change successfully mitigated impact. 

Scope of impact: Any user attempting to access Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat using the Edge browser could have experienced intermittent impact. 

Start time: Thursday, April 23, 2026, at 12:00 PM UTC

End time: Tuesday, April 28, 2026, at 10:00 PM UTC

Root cause: A recent regression in the Office Home sign-in experience was introduced as part of the Proactive Authentication feature rollout, which enables Microsoft Edge to perform silent, preemptive sign-in functionality. As part of this feature, a new ‘EdgeAuth’ cookie was added by the browser, containing an encrypted, signed, and Base64-encoded Azure Active Directory (AAD) access token to support non-interactive authentication flows.

Even prior to this change, one of the existing Auth cookies used by Office Home was already near platform size due to multiple factors, including encryption overheads. The addition of the ‘EdgeAuth’ cookie on top of existing other cookies caused the total request header size to exceed Azure Front Door (AFD) limits, resulting in intermittent “Request Header Field Too Long” errors and blocking access to Microsoft 365 Copilot.
 
Next steps: 
- For a more comprehensive list of next steps and actions, please refer to the Post Incident Report document.

We'll provide a preliminary Post-Incident Report within two business days and a final Post-Incident Report within five business days.
